Name: CACL_CORRECT_GRAPH_ST
Description: Provides growth functions for various mechanics. Handles all stat scaling.

Fields.Name: Threshold Point [0]
Fields.Description: Stat Level Cap corresponds to the level of a certain stat

Fields.Name: Threshold Point [1]
Fields.Description: Stage cap corresponds to the level of a certain stat

Fields.Name: Threshold Point [2]
Fields.Description: Stage cap corresponds to the level of a certain stat

Fields.Name: Threshold Point [3]
Fields.Description: Stage cap corresponds to the level of a certain stat

Fields.Name: Threshold Point [4]
Fields.Description: Stage cap corresponds to the level of a certain stat

Fields.Name: Threshold Coefficient [0]
Fields.Description:
Stage growth is a multiplier of the maximum growth that will occur in a certain stage, as an example a chart such as this:
Growth Stage 0 - Growth = 20 - 20%% of the total scaling is reached at stage 0.
Growth Stage 1 - Growth = 25 - 25%% of the total scaling is reached at stage 1.
Growth Stage 2 - Growth = 50 - 50%% of the total scaling is reached at stage 2.
Growth Stage 3 - Growth = 80 - 80%% of the total scaling is reached at stage 3.
Growth Stage 4 - Growth = 90 - 90%% of the total scaling is reached at stage 4.
As you can see, in this specific chart 100%% is never reached, 100%% could be reached if only the value of growth stage 4 was 100%
This is not limited to 100%% as an example growth stage 4 could reach 200%%.
The level cap of a growth stage is specified in the corresponding Stat Level Cap Field

Fields.Name: Adjustment Factor [0]
Fields.Description:
Determines the exponent used to calculate the curve.
The growth values are normalised to be between the level range and the growth range dictated,
Such that: 
0 level is the lower bound of the stage, and 1 level is the upper bound
0 growth is the lower bound of the stage, and 1 growth is the upper bound
This curve is, at a value of 1, linear, meaning direct proportion between each stage.
At a value of 2, it is quadratic (in fact, it will be flat exactly at 0, and have steepness 2 at 1)
Note that a steepness of 2 means the growth per level is double the average growth per level.
At a value of 0.5, it is a square root.
Below 0, negative powers are not used as this would create asymptotes and infinite values.
Instead, the axes are flipped. If you consider the graph 0 to 1 by 0 to 1, it is rotated 180 degrees.
At a value of -1, it is linear again.
At a value of -2, it is quadratic, however the origin is at the upper bound.
Note this means there is a steepness of 2 at the lower bound and it is flat at the upper bound.
At a value of -0.5, it is a square root graph again, but rotated 180.

Fields.Name: Leveling Cost - Initial Cost
Fields.Description: Growth Soul Slope of the early graph 1

Fields.Name: Leveling Cost - Cost Increase
Fields.Description: Affects the slope of the graph after the growth soul threshold 3
